summary_one_line: Input must contain a 4-byte OpenType script tag (e.g. GPOS/GSUB/latn) at a position where find_script_index matches the table's script list.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte OpenType script tag (FOURCC such as 'GPOS', 'GSUB', 'latn') present in the input at offsets that flow into script_tags[i], so that g.find_script_index matches an entry in the parsed table's script list and the true branch at line 557 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry literal ASCII FOURCC tags: at offsets 0x0c-0x0f the W bytes are concentrated on G/S/U/O/B/P producing 'GPOS' and 'GSUB', and seeds 2/4/5 carry the 'latn' tag at 0x18-0x1b; these exact tag values let find_script_index match and resolve the lookup (W takes the true side T=12 at line 557).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have no concentrated tag at those offsets (0x0c is 00/01/20/2c, 0x0d-0x0f are scattered non-tag bytes), so find_script_index never matches at line 557 (L shows T=0 F=86) and the lookup falls through to the DFLT/dflt/latn fallbacks that all return false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the per-table tag comparison inside find_script_index and record both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the real table script tag into the input at the matching offset, manufacturing the exact FOURCC that naive/value_profile must stumble onto by chance and rarely do.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a find_script_index call gated on script_tags[i].
Both decisive pairs are I2S-delta pairs separating cmplog/vpc from their non-I2S counterparts.
The second decisive pair is cmplog over naive, also an I2S delta.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII FOURCC tags GPOS and GSUB.
A winner-resolving cmplog seed carries the GSUB tag at offset 0x0c.
Winner cmplog seeds also carry the 'latn' script tag at offset 0x18.
At the FOURCC offsets the W byte sets are concentrated on tag letters while the L byte sets are scattered non-tag bytes.
The winner takes the true side at the blocker line while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 557  T=12 F=28  T=0 F=86  if (g.find_script_index (script_tags[i], script_index))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog records both CMP operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer and I2SRandReplace substitutes one operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it has no operand-substitution path.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5560 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed de55a8859d5a2153` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -, ancestor e0a5b09f80625fb6) at depth 19 of the 20-level chain; tool printed 'I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 19'.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating find_script_index on a 4-byte script tag where cmplog/vpc resolve the branch no faster than naive/value_profile (no cmplog>naive or vpc>vp separation), which would refute that I2S tag substitution is the enabling mechanism.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset where the matched tag flows into script_tags[i] is inferred from byte concentration rather than confirmed by dataflow, since the script_tags array is populated by hb_ot_tags_from_script_and_language upstream of the parsed font bytes.
